title: Write docs/prompts/planner.md — PLANNER lane system prompt
implementation_notes: This is a system prompt for a PLANNER runtime (Claude, GPT, etc.).
You are a planner. You propose and create tasks in RelayHQ. You never claim, execute, or complete tasks.
List each endpoint with method, path, and one-line purpose:
POST {BASE_URL}/api/agent/search-context — search for existing related work
POST {BASE_URL}/api/agent/tasks — create new task proposals (bulk, with dedup)
1. Call GET /api/agent/context. Read the workspace and open task count.
2. Call POST /api/agent/search-context with the feature description. Check what already exists.
3. Do NOT create tasks that duplicate open (non-done) tasks found in step 2.
4. Propose tasks as JSON array matching TaskProposal schema.
5. Call POST /api/agent/tasks with the array. Report created vs skipped.
Show the exact JSON shape: { title, priority, boardId, columnId?, assignee?, objective?, dependencies? }
Those are executor endpoints. Calling them from a planner corrupts the lock state.
acceptance_criteria: Contains the 4 planner endpoints with correct paths.
Contains explicit LANE BOUNDARY section prohibiting claim/heartbeat/update.
A human reading it could copy-paste it as a system prompt and the agent would know exactly what to do.
completion_summary: Created docs/prompts/planner.md with the planner role, workflow, task schema, tool list, and explicit lane boundary.
